next_steps: Send cerebrospinal fluid for Angiostrongylus cantonensis PCR
Send serum and cerebrospinal fluid for serologic testing for A. cantonensis, Gnathostoma, and Coccidioides
Perform brain and spine MRI with contrast
Continue current empiric antimicrobial therapy until bacterial meningitis is ruled out
Consult infectious disease and neurology specialists
Carefully monitor intracranial pressure and neurologic status
Perform MRI of the brain and entire spine with gadolinium contrast to identify nerve root enhancement or larval migration tracts
Send cerebrospinal fluid for Angiostrongylus cantonensis PCR and specialized parasitic panels
Perform a detailed ophthalmologic examination (slit lamp) to rule out ocular larval migration
Obtain serologic testing for A. cantonensis, Gnathostoma, and relevant helminths
Continue current empiric antimicrobial therapy until bacterial meningitis and other pathogens are definitively ruled out
Consult infectious disease and neurology for ongoing management and potential use of antiparasitic agents
Inquire specifically about the ingestion of raw, undercooked, or inadequately washed produce (potential slug/snail vectors) in Hawaii
